versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: 1 This is what the LORD says:
“Where is your mother’s certificate of divorce
with which I sent her away?
Or to which of My creditors
Look, you were sold for your iniquities,
and for your transgressions your mother was sent away.
2 Why was no one there when I arrived?
Why did no one answer when I called?
Is My hand too short to redeem you?
Or do I lack the strength to deliver you?
Behold, My rebuke dries up the sea;
I turn the rivers into a desert;
the fish rot for lack of water
3 I clothe the heavens in black

context_lines: In **Isaiah 50**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Isaiah.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **Israel’s Sin** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Isaiah 50** remains the first control on meaning.
